_about: AQE MCP governance posture, snapshotted for `harness mcp-scan` (plan 05 / A1). Default-deny is ENFORCED by src/mcp/tool-scoping.ts (isToolAllowed falls through to `return false`; only fleet-admin/unrestricted carry allowAll). This file is the declarative mirror + CI gate input. See _enforcement for what is wired vs stated-intent.

acceptedRisks.allowAll-admin-tiers: fleet-admin and unrestricted carry allowAll by design (admin/orchestration tiers). They are NOT host-exposed default roles; agents are scoped to a named non-admin role. Tracked for future tightening of `unrestricted`.
acceptedRisks.unpinned-app-deps: package.json carries caret/tilde ranges (21/23) — normal for a 23-dep application; the scan's dep-pinning check targets minted single-purpose harnesses, N/A here.

_enforcement.defaultDeny: ENFORCED — src/mcp/tool-scoping.ts isToolAllowed() returns false unless a role's allowlist (or allowAll) matches.
_enforcement.roles: ENFORCED — mirrors DEFAULT_SCOPES in src/mcp/tool-scoping.ts (kept in sync; see mcp-policy-gate test).
_enforcement.allowShell: POSTURE re: the EXPOSED tool surface — no AQE QE tool grants the model arbitrary command execution (the surface is test_generate/coverage_analyze/etc., not a generic exec/terminal tool). The QE tool RUNTIME does spawn SCOPED test subprocesses (node --test, mutation runs); that is gated, not arbitrary. (The bundled ruflo server in .mcp.json is a separate governance domain with its own shell-capable agents.)
_enforcement.allowNetwork/allowFileWrite: POSTURE — QE MCP tools operate on the local workspace and return data; outbound model calls go through the host routing layer, not tool-granted capabilities.
_enforcement.toolTimeoutMs/maxToolCallsPerTurn/requir: STATED — declared bounds; host enforcement is the follow-up wiring.
